codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original handoff/transcript plus the later startup-prewarm fixes and current branch code. Although the original handoff cited an unrelated commit, the current combined startup-prewarm behavior is now covered by commits f5bde92, 84c94b0, and fd3c6ff: prewarm targets and budget are declared in code, diagnostics and Prometheus metrics surface target results and first-request evidence, non-timeout failures are classified distinctly while later targets still run, and current warm-path attribution requires normalized target equivalence rather than pathname-only matching. No unresolved correctness issue remains against VAL-OPS-006 in the combined current behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The original startup-prewarm worker successfully validated inherited branch state but ended the handoff with `rev-parse HEAD`, which pointed to an unrelated operational-controls artifact commit instead of a feature-attributable diff. Reviewers had to inspect later fix features and current code to recover the actual evidence chain.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl session 87156602-5e6c-43db-a459-95a613c382e3 ends with commit c0c00e96768e00aa6ee85c9f5b913ec7b8a2d665, while `git show --stat c0c00e96768e00aa6ee85c9f5b913ec7b8a2d665` only contains .factory/validation/operational-controls/user-testing artifacts. The actual startup-prewarm implementation and fixes are attributable to f5bde92, 84c94b0, and fd3c6ff.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the original prewarm handoff, later fix reviews, transcript evidence, and current source/tests. Per the orchestrator note, the combined current startup-prewarm behavior now satisfies VAL-OPS-006: declared hot targets are prewarmed within a configured budget, diagnostics/metrics expose the behavior, handler failures are classified correctly without aborting later targets, and no blocking correctness issue remains in the current implementation.
